summary: Iteration 12 backend testing: audit log + SEO ping/IndexNow features. All 16 tests passed (100%). RBAC on GET /api/audit enforced (401 unauth / 403 sub-admin even with seo / 200 superadmin). Mutating actions (login, client create+delete, service create, document create) produce correct audit entries with expected shape (user_email, action, entity, created_at) and no password_hash leaks. Filters ?entity= and ?action= work. POST /api/seo/ping gated by 'seo' permission (403 for sub-admin without it, 200 for both superadmin and sub-admin with it); returns {base_url, results{google,bing,indexnow}}. IndexNow key file 404 without config, serves key text when configured, 404 for wrong key. Settings sanitizes ai_own_key/email_api_key/slack_webhook_url/stytch_secret and exposes has_* booleans; search_engine_ping_enabled and indexnow_key readable/writable. Regression sanity (superadmin login, /clients RBAC, sitemap.xml, robots.txt) all pass. All mutated settings and temp sub-admins were cleaned up.
critical_code_review_comments: core/seo_ping.py uses blocking `requests` inside asyncio.to_thread which is fine, but consider httpx.AsyncClient for consistency.
GET /api/audit sorts by created_at (ISO string) — works because now_iso() is lexicographically sortable UTC, but a real datetime index would be more robust for high-volume audit logs.
audit.log_action swallows all exceptions (by design) — good, but consider a metrics counter so silent failures aren't invisible.
settings PUT accepts arbitrary payload keys via `$set` (no whitelist) — combined with sanitize-on-read this is OK, but an unknown/malformed key can persist forever in Mongo settings doc.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted throwaway sub-admins (TEST_noseo_*, TEST_seo_*, TEST_nocrm_*) and temp client/service/document with TEST_ prefixes. All cleaned up. indexnow_key and search_engine_ping_enabled reset to '' / False.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 12 audit + seo-ping backend verified passing 100%. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iter12_audit_seo.py. External ping targets (Google/Bing/IndexNow) may or may not be reachable from sandbox; the endpoint correctly returns 200 with per-target statuses regardless.
